body_txt: This Super-Trope describes situations where a relationship that isn't romantic is still seen as romantic by another character. The key to this trope is that two people can have some sort of relationship without it being romantic. Neither of the two characters have (or probably ever will have) any interest in the relationship becoming romantic, unlike in the Just Friends trope which requires that to happen with friendships. However, one or more bystanders/others think that the relationship is a romantic one and may try to call them out on it. The two may be (but are not necessarily) Heterosexual Life-Partners , Platonic Life-Partners , or Like Brother and Sister , or they could simply be blood relatives or business partners. If a third person gets involved with one of the people and then finds out about the other it can turn into an Imaginary Love Triangle . See also The Not-Love Interest , a character who fills a typical Love Interest role but isn't actually interested in romancing the protagonist or vice versa. Contrast She Is Not My Girlfriend and Everyone Can See It : the participants in this trope are not remotely romantically interested in each other, it is all in the eyes of the beholders. In those tropes, the bystanders are being more perceptive and there is some truth in it.  See also Mistaken for Flirting . For cases where audience members interpret platonic interactions of characters in the work through the lens of Shipping , see Shipping Goggles . NOTE: This is a love trope so there may be unhidden spoilers ahead. noreallife
